title: Tanacetum yoshinaganthum
intro: Tanacetum yoshinaganthum, commonly known as Japanese tansy, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, known for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Tanacetum yoshinaganthum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and menstrual disorders.
